properties.name: print-color-adjust
properties.description: Controls how colors are handled when printing. The print-color-adjust CSS property sets what, if anything, the user agent may do to optimize the appearance of the element on the output device.

values.name: exact
values.description: Colors should be printed exactly as specified in the document, even if this requires more ink or toner.

values.name: economy
values.description: The user agent may make adjustments to the element as it deems appropriate to optimize the output for the device it is being printed to.

properties.name: -webkit-print-color-adjust
properties.description: WebKit vendor prefix for print-color-adjust. Controls how colors are handled when printing in WebKit-based browsers.

values.name: exact
values.description: Colors should be printed exactly as specified in the document.

values.name: economy
values.description: The browser may make adjustments to optimize printing.

atDirectives.name: @page
atDirectives.description: The @page CSS at-rule is used to modify different aspects of a printed page.

pseudoClasses.name: :first
pseudoClasses.description: The :first pseudo-class matches the first page when printing a document.

pseudoClasses.name: :left
pseudoClasses.description: The :left pseudo-class matches all left pages when printing a document.

pseudoClasses.name: :right
pseudoClasses.description: The :right pseudo-class matches all right pages when printing a document.
